# yaml-language-server: $schema=https://raw.githubusercontent.com/redhat-developer/rhdh-plugins/refs/heads/main/workspaces/extensions/json-schema/plugins.json
apiVersion: extensions.backstage.io/v1alpha1
kind: Plugin
metadata:
  name: home-page
  namespace: rhdh
  title: Dynamic Home Page
  description: |
    Customize the home page experience of Red Hat Developer Hub for your users. Display important information, announcements, and links to resources, systems, etc.
  annotations:
    extensions.backstage.io/pre-installed: 'true' # this means the plugin yaml is preinstalled, not the plugin itself, all other plugins are marked as 'custom'
    extensions.backstage.io/verified-by: Red Hat
  tags: 
    - customization
  links:
    - url: https://red.ht/rhdh
      title: Homepage
    - url: https://issues.redhat.com/browse/RHDHBUGS
      title: "Report issues here. Note: Customers of Red Hat Developer Hub should raise a Red Hat support case instead."
    - title: Documentation for Red Hat Developer Hub
      url: https://docs.redhat.com/en/documentation/red_hat_developer_hub/latest/html/customizing_red_hat_developer_hub/customizing-the-home-page
    - title: Source Code
      url: https://github.com/redhat-developer/rhdh-plugins/tree/main/workspaces/homepage
    - title: Red Hat Developer Hub Life Cycle Support Policy
      url: https://access.redhat.com/support/policy/updates/developerhub
spec:
  author: Red Hat
  support:
    provider: Red Hat
    level: generally-available
  lifecycle: active
  publisher: Red Hat

  categories:
    - Customization

  highlights:
    - Customize the home page of Red Hat Developer Hub

  description: |
    This plugin ships as enabled by default in Red Hat Developer Hub.
    
    Home Page is a plugin that allows you to customize the home page of Red Hat Developer Hub. 
    The home page is the first page that users see when they log in to Red Hat Developer Hub. 
    You can use this plugin to add custom content to the home page, such as links to important resources, 
    announcements, or other information that you want to share with users. 
    
    The home page can be customized using a simple configuration file that allows you to define sections 
    with different types of content, such as text, links, images, and more.

    ## Adding The Plugin To Red Hat Developer Hub

    See the [Red Hat Developer Hub documentation](https://docs.redhat.com/en/documentation/red_hat_developer_hub/latest/html/installing_and_viewing_plugins_in_red_hat_developer_hub/extensions-in-rhdh_plugins-in-rhdh) 
    for further instructions on how to add, enable, configure, and remove plugins in your instance.

    ## Configuring The Plugin

    Plugins often need additional configuration to work correctly - particularly those that integrate with other 
    systems. See the original source code repository, the software vendor, or the [Red Hat Developer Hub documentation](https://docs.redhat.com/en/documentation/red_hat_developer_hub/latest/html/installing_and_viewing_plugins_in_red_hat_developer_hub)
    for further details regarding the configuration required.

  # Images are base 64 encoded SVGs (below is a blank square from the mockup)
  icon: data:image/svg+xml;base64,
    

  packages:
    - red-hat-developer-hub-backstage-plugin-dynamic-home-page
    - red-hat-developer-hub-backstage-plugin-homepage-backend
  installation: |
      ## Installation

      ### Dynamic Plugin Installation for Red Hat Developer Hub

      Add the following configuration to your dynamic plugin yaml. Use the OCI image tags from the Dynamic Home Page and Homepage Backend package entries in the Extensions catalog for your RHDH version if they differ from the examples below.

      ```yaml
      includes:
        - dynamic-plugins.default.yaml
      plugins:
        # Frontend plugin
        - package: ./dynamic-plugins/dist/red-hat-developer-hub-backstage-plugin-dynamic-home-page
          enabled: true
          pluginConfig:
            dynamicPlugins:
              frontend:
                red-hat-developer-hub.backstage-plugin-dynamic-home-page:
                  dynamicRoutes:
                    - path: /
                      importName: DynamicHomePage
                  mountPoints:
                    - mountPoint: application/listener
                      importName: VisitListener
                    - mountPoint: home.page/cards
                      importName: OnboardingSection
                      config:
                        id: rhdh-onboarding-section
                        title: Red Hat Developer Hub - Onboarding
                        cardLayout:
                          width:
                            minColumns: 4
                            maxColumns: 12
                            defaultColumns: 12
                          height:
                            minRows: 2
                            maxRows: 12
                            defaultRows: 4
                        layouts:
                          xl:
                            w: 12
                            h: 6
                          lg:
                            w: 12
                            h: 6
                          md:
                            w: 12
                            h: 7
                          sm:
                            w: 12
                            h: 8
                          xs:
                            w: 12
                            h: 9
                          xxs:
                            w: 12
                            h: 14
                    - mountPoint: home.page/cards
                      importName: EntitySection
                      config:
                        id: rhdh-entity-section
                        title: Red Hat Developer Hub - Software Catalog
                        cardLayout:
                          width:
                            minColumns: 4
                            maxColumns: 12
                            defaultColumns: 12
                          height:
                            minRows: 2
                            maxRows: 12
                            defaultRows: 4
                        layouts:
                          xl:
                            w: 12
                            h: 7
                          lg:
                            w: 12
                            h: 7
                          md:
                            w: 12
                            h: 8
                          sm:
                            w: 12
                            h: 9
                          xs:
                            w: 12
                            h: 11
                          xxs:
                            w: 12
                            h: 15
                    - mountPoint: home.page/cards
                      importName: TemplateSection
                      config:
                        id: rhdh-template-section
                        title: Red Hat Developer Hub - Explore templates
                        cardLayout:
                          width:
                            minColumns: 4
                            maxColumns: 12
                            defaultColumns: 12
                          height:
                            minRows: 2
                            maxRows: 12
                            defaultRows: 4
                        layouts:
                          xl:
                            w: 12
                            h: 5
                          lg:
                            w: 12
                            h: 5
                          md:
                            w: 12
                            h: 5
                          sm:
                            w: 12
                            h: 5
                          xs:
                            w: 12
                            h: 7.5
                          xxs:
                            w: 12
                            h: 13.5
                    - mountPoint: home.page/cards
                      importName: QuickAccessCard
                      config:
                        id: quickaccess-card
                        title: Quick Access Card
                    - mountPoint: home.page/cards
                      importName: SearchBar
                      config:
                        id: searchbar
                        title: Search
                        cardLayout:
                          width:
                            minColumns: 4
                            maxColumns: 12
                            defaultColumns: 12
                          height:
                            minRows: 1
                            maxRows: 2
                            defaultRows: 2
                    - mountPoint: home.page/cards
                      importName: CatalogStarredEntitiesCard
                      config:
                        id: catalog-starred-entities-card
                        title: Starred catalog entities
                    - mountPoint: home.page/cards
                      importName: RecentlyVisitedCard
                      config:
                        id: recently-visited-card
                        title: Recently visited
                    - mountPoint: home.page/cards
                      importName: TopVisitedCard
                      config:
                        id: top-visited-card
                        title: Top visited
                    - mountPoint: home.page/cards
                      importName: FeaturedDocsCard
                      config:
                        id: featured-docs-card
                        title: Featured docs
                    - mountPoint: home.page/cards
                      importName: JokeCard
                      config:
                        id: joke-card
                        title: Random joke
                  translationResources:
                    - importName: homepageTranslations
                      ref: homepageTranslationRef
        # Backend plugin
        - package: oci://ghcr.io/redhat-developer/rhdh-plugin-export-overlays/red-hat-developer-hub-backstage-plugin-homepage-backend:bs_1.49.4__0.1.0!red-hat-developer-hub-backstage-plugin-homepage-backend
          enabled: true
          pluginConfig:
            homepage:
              defaultWidgets:
                - id: rhdh-onboarding-section
                  props:
                    title: Onboarding section
                - id: rhdh-entity-section
                  props:
                    title: Entity section
                - id: rhdh-template-section
                  props:
                    title: Template section
      ```

input.content:
# RHIDP-14515: Rename homepage frontend plugin from dynamic-home-page to homepage

- **Jira:** https://redhat.atlassian.net/browse/RHIDP-14515
- **Type:** Story (3 SP)
- **Status:** Review
- **Assignee:** Christoph Jerolimov
- **Team:** RHDH Frontend Plugins & UI

## Background

The homepage frontend plugin is currently named `dynamic-home-page` — a legacy name from when it was a thin wrapper around the upstream Backstage `home` plugin with added dynamic mount points. With RHDH 2.x / NFS, the scalprum dependencies and mount point code are no longer needed. The backend counterpart is already named `homepage-backend`, so the frontend should be aligned to just `homepage`.

The plugin ID also changes from `home` to `homepage` so it can be installed alongside the upstream home plugin.

## Scope

This plan covers **only the overlay repo** (`rhdh-plugin-export-overlays`). The upstream source changes in `rhdh-plugins` are a prerequisite and must land first.

## Prerequisites (upstream — rhdh-plugins repo)

- [ ] Rename npm package from `@red-hat-developer-hub/backstage-plugin-dynamic-home-page` to `@red-hat-developer-hub/backstage-plugin-homepage`
- [ ] Change plugin ID from `home` to `homepage`
- [ ] Remove scalprum dependencies and all mount point related code
- [ ] Remove OFS-related test apps, unit tests, and Playwright tests if NFS equivalents exist
- [ ] Publish the renamed package

## Plan — Overlay Repo Changes

### 1. Update `plugins-list.yaml`

**File:** `workspaces/homepage/plugins-list.yaml`

Rename the plugin entry:
```
- plugins/dynamic-home-page  →  plugins/homepage
+ plugins/homepage:
```

### 2. Rename overlay directory

**Current:** `workspaces/homepage/plugins/dynamic-home-page/`
**New:** `workspaces/homepage/plugins/homepage/`

Contains `scalprum-config.json` — this file may need to be removed entirely if scalprum is no longer needed with NFS, or updated with the new package name:
- Current scalprum name: `red-hat-developer-hub.backstage-plugin-dynamic-home-page`
- New scalprum name: `red-hat-developer-hub.backstage-plugin-homepage` (if still needed)

### 3. Rename and update Package metadata

**Current file:** `workspaces/homepage/metadata/red-hat-developer-hub-backstage-plugin-dynamic-home-page.yaml`
**New file:** `workspaces/homepage/metadata/red-hat-developer-hub-backstage-plugin-homepage.yaml`

Changes inside:
- `metadata.name`: `red-hat-developer-hub-backstage-plugin-dynamic-home-page` → `red-hat-developer-hub-backstage-plugin-homepage`
- `spec.packageName`: `@red-hat-developer-hub/backstage-plugin-dynamic-home-page` → `@red-hat-developer-hub/backstage-plugin-homepage`
- `spec.dynamicArtifact`: update dist path reference
- `spec.appConfigExamples`: update all `red-hat-developer-hub.backstage-plugin-dynamic-home-page` references → `red-hat-developer-hub.backstage-plugin-homepage`
- Remove mount point configuration from `appConfigExamples` (no longer needed with NFS)
- Update `dynamicRoutes` and `translationResources` if import names change

### 4. Update Plugin catalog entity

**File:** `catalog-entities/extensions/plugins/home-page.yaml`

- Update `spec.packages` list: `red-hat-developer-hub-backstage-plugin-dynamic-home-page` → `red-hat-developer-hub-backstage-plugin-homepage`
- Update `spec.installation` section: all `dynamic-home-page` references → `homepage`
- Update title from "Dynamic Home Page" to "Homepage" (align with new naming)

### 5. Update `default.packages.yaml`

**File:** `default.packages.yaml` (line 24)

```
- package: '@red-hat-developer-hub/backstage-plugin-dynamic-home-page'
+ package: '@red-hat-developer-hub/backstage-plugin-homepage'
```

### 6. Update `rhdh-supported-packages.txt`

**File:** `rhdh-supported-packages.txt` (line 26)

```
- homepage/plugins/dynamic-home-page
+ homepage/plugins/homepage
```

### 7. Update E2E tests

**Files in `workspaces/homepage/e2e-tests/`:**

- `tests/config/dynamic-plugins.yaml` — update package paths and plugin config keys from `dynamic-home-page` to `homepage`
- `tests/specs/dynamic-home-page.spec.ts` — rename file to `homepage.spec.ts`, update internal references (`DYNAMIC_HOME_PAGE_WRAPPER_DIST_NAMES`, etc.)
- `tests/utils/dynamic-homepage.ts` — update any `dynamic-home-page` references
- `playwright.config.ts` — update `testMatch` if it references the old spec file name

### 8. Update scorecard E2E tests (cross-workspace dependency)

**Files:**
- `workspaces/scorecard/e2e-tests/tests/config/dynamic-plugins.yaml` — update homepage plugin references (lines 127, 132, 147)
- `workspaces/scorecard/e2e-tests/tests/config/filecheck/dynamic-plugins.yaml` — update homepage plugin references (lines 52, 57, 72)

### 9. Verify `source.json` and `repo-ref`

**File:** `workspaces/homepage/source.json`

Update `repo-ref` to the commit/tag that contains the renamed package in the upstream rhdh-plugins repo.

## Post-merge Checklist

- [ ] Run `/publish` on the PR to build test OCI images with the new name
- [ ] Run `/smoketest` to verify the renamed plugin loads correctly
- [ ] Ping COPE team (Nick, Karthik) to verify Konflux build jobs are updated for the new package name
- [ ] Check [Konflux onboarding docs](https://gitlab.cee.redhat.com/rhidp/rhdh-plugin-catalog/-/blob/rhdh-1-rhel-9/docs/ONBOARD_KONFLUX.adoc) — the rename may require onboarding the new name as a "new plugin"
- [ ] Check RHDH documentation for any `dynamic-home-page` references that need updating
- [ ] Verify QE test suites are updated for the new plugin name
- [ ] Include in technical enablement / demo materials

## Risk Notes

- The package rename may be treated as a "new plugin" by Konflux, requiring new build pipeline configuration rather than just updating an existing one.
- Other workspaces (scorecard) have cross-references to the homepage plugin in their E2E configs — these must be updated in the same PR or coordinated.
- Release branches (`release-x.y`) should NOT receive this rename — it's a `main`-only change for the next major release.

toolUseResult.originalFile:
# RHIDP-14515: Rename homepage frontend plugin from dynamic-home-page to homepage

- **Jira:** https://redhat.atlassian.net/browse/RHIDP-14515
- **Type:** Story (3 SP)
- **Status:** Review
- **Assignee:** Christoph Jerolimov
- **Team:** RHDH Frontend Plugins & UI

## Background

The homepage frontend plugin is currently named `dynamic-home-page` — a legacy name from when it was a thin wrapper around the upstream Backstage `home` plugin with added dynamic mount points. With RHDH 2.x / NFS, the scalprum dependencies and mount point code are no longer needed. The backend counterpart is already named `homepage-backend`, so the frontend should be aligned to just `homepage`.

The plugin ID also changes from `home` to `homepage` so it can be installed alongside the upstream home plugin.

All upstream prerequisites in rhdh-plugins are already complete (package renamed, plugin ID changed, scalprum removed, OFS tests removed, package published).

## Scope

This plan covers **only the overlay repo** (`rhdh-plugin-export-overlays`). Scorecard E2E test updates are out of scope and will be handled in a follow-up PR.

## Plan — Overlay Repo Changes

### 1. Update `source.json` and `repo-ref`

**File:** `workspaces/homepage/source.json`

Update `repo-ref` to the latest commit/tag in upstream rhdh-plugins that contains the renamed package. This must happen first so that subsequent builds pick up the correct source.

### 2. Update `plugins-list.yaml`

**File:** `workspaces/homepage/plugins-list.yaml`

Rename the plugin entry:
```
- plugins/dynamic-home-page  →  plugins/homepage
+ plugins/homepage:
```

### 3. Remove old overlay directory, no replacement needed

**Remove:** `workspaces/homepage/plugins/dynamic-home-page/` (contains only `scalprum-config.json`)

The scalprum config is no longer needed with NFS — delete the entire directory.

### 4. Rename and update Package metadata (frontend)

**Current file:** `workspaces/homepage/metadata/red-hat-developer-hub-backstage-plugin-dynamic-home-page.yaml`
**New file:** `workspaces/homepage/metadata/red-hat-developer-hub-backstage-plugin-homepage.yaml`

Changes inside:
- `metadata.name`: `red-hat-developer-hub-backstage-plugin-dynamic-home-page` → `red-hat-developer-hub-backstage-plugin-homepage`
- `metadata.title`: `Dynamic Home Page` → `Homepage`
- `spec.packageName`: `@red-hat-developer-hub/backstage-plugin-dynamic-home-page` → `@red-hat-developer-hub/backstage-plugin-homepage`
- `spec.dynamicArtifact`: update dist path reference
- `spec.version`: update to latest released version from rhdh-plugins
- `spec.appConfigExamples`: update all `red-hat-developer-hub.backstage-plugin-dynamic-home-page` references → `red-hat-developer-hub.backstage-plugin-homepage`
- Remove mount point configuration from `appConfigExamples` (no longer needed with NFS)
- Update `dynamicRoutes` and `translationResources` if import names change

### 5. Update Package metadata (backend) — version bump

**File:** `workspaces/homepage/metadata/red-hat-developer-hub-backstage-plugin-homepage-backend.yaml`

- `spec.version`: update to latest released version from rhdh-plugins
- `spec.dynamicArtifact`: update OCI tag to match the new version

### 6. Update Plugin catalog entity

**File:** `catalog-entities/extensions/plugins/home-page.yaml`

- Update `spec.packages` list: `red-hat-developer-hub-backstage-plugin-dynamic-home-page` → `red-hat-developer-hub-backstage-plugin-homepage`
- Update `spec.installation` section: all `dynamic-home-page` references → `homepage`
- Update title from "Dynamic Home Page" to "Homepage" (align with new naming)

### 7. Update `default.packages.yaml`

**File:** `default.packages.yaml` (line 24)

```
- package: '@red-hat-developer-hub/backstage-plugin-dynamic-home-page'
+ package: '@red-hat-developer-hub/backstage-plugin-homepage'
```

### 8. Update `rhdh-supported-packages.txt`

**File:** `rhdh-supported-packages.txt` (line 26)

```
- homepage/plugins/dynamic-home-page
+ homepage/plugins/homepage
```

### 9. Update E2E tests

**Files in `workspaces/homepage/e2e-tests/`:**

- `tests/config/dynamic-plugins.yaml` — update package paths and plugin config keys from `dynamic-home-page` to `homepage`
- `tests/specs/dynamic-home-page.spec.ts` — rename file to `homepage.spec.ts`, update internal references (`DYNAMIC_HOME_PAGE_WRAPPER_DIST_NAMES`, etc.)
- `tests/utils/dynamic-homepage.ts` — update any `dynamic-home-page` references
- `playwright.config.ts` — update `testMatch` if it references the old spec file name

## Follow-up PR (out of scope)

- Update scorecard E2E tests that cross-reference the homepage plugin:
  - `workspaces/scorecard/e2e-tests/tests/config/dynamic-plugins.yaml`
  - `workspaces/scorecard/e2e-tests/tests/config/filecheck/dynamic-plugins.yaml`

## Post-merge Checklist

- [ ] Run `/publish` on the PR to build test OCI images with the new name
- [ ] Run `/smoketest` to verify the renamed plugin loads correctly
- [ ] Ping COPE team (Nick, Karthik) to verify Konflux build jobs are updated for the new package name
- [ ] Check [Konflux onboarding docs](https://gitlab.cee.redhat.com/rhidp/rhdh-plugin-catalog/-/blob/rhdh-1-rhel-9/docs/ONBOARD_KONFLUX.adoc) — the rename may require onboarding the new name as a "new plugin"
- [ ] Check RHDH documentation for any `dynamic-home-page` references that need updating
- [ ] Verify QE test suites are updated for the new plugin name
- [ ] Include in technical enablement / demo materials

## Risk Notes

- The package rename may be treated as a "new plugin" by Konflux, requiring new build pipeline configuration rather than just updating an existing one.
- Scorecard E2E tests have cross-references to the homepage plugin — the follow-up PR must land before scorecard tests can pass.
- Release branches (`release-x.y`) should NOT receive this rename — it's a `main`-only change for the next major release.

      ## Installation

      ### Dynamic Plugin Installation for Red Hat Developer Hub

      Add the following configuration to your dynamic plugin yaml. Use the OCI image tags from the Dynamic Home Page and Homepage Backend package entries in the Extensions catalog for your RHDH version if they differ from the examples below.

      ```yaml
      includes:
        - dynamic-plugins.default.yaml
      plugins:

content.content:
[Publish workflow](https://github.com/redhat-developer/rhdh-plugin-export-overlays/actions/runs/28864944508) has completed with success. #### Publishing process ✅ Finished successfully. ✅ Published container images: - ghcr.io/redhat-developer/rhdh-plugin-export-overlays/red-hat-developer-hub-backstage-plugin-homepage:pr_2644__1.15.0 - ghcr.io/redhat-developer/rhdh-plugin-export-overlays/red-hat-developer-hub-backstage-plugin-homepage-backend:pr_2644__0.2.1 #### Backstage-incompatible workspaces Some workspaces have a backstage version (in sources or in their overlay folder) which is *__incompatible__* with the target Backstage version (`1.52.0`). 1 incompatible workspaces, 0 of which are mandatory: | Folder | Backstage version | Mandatory | Info | |--------|-------------------|-----------|-------| | [workspaces/homepage](https://github.com/christoph-jerolimov/rhdh-plugin-export-overlays/tree/homepage/rename-package/workspaces/homepage) | 1.51.0 | :white_circle: | [BS Version History](https://github.com/redhat-developer/rhdh-plugins/commits/main/workspaces/homepage/backstage.json) |
How to fix You have 3 main options: - *__Use the already-opened PR :__* For automatically-discovered workspaces, there might already be an automatically-opened PR that updates the commit to the target backstage version (`Automatic PR` link in column 4 above): just publish the plugins and test them from the PR by following the PR instructions. - *__Manually update the workspace commit :__* - Find a newer commit, with a backstage version compatible with the target backstage version :__* - *__Backstage version history available__*: A `BS Version History` link might be available in column 4: in the workspace source repository, it points to the list of commits touching the `backstage.json` file. This would help you finding out whether this workspace has a commit that has been published for the target backstage version. - *__No info available__*: If no information is is provided in column 4, the `backstage.json` probably does not exist in workspace sources. You would have to look deeper into the newer commits of workspace sources, possibly in the low-level dependencies in the `package.json` files, to find out a commit with an underlying backstage version compatible with the target backstage version. - Then, in the overlay repository, update the `repo-ref` field of the `source.json` file to the new source commit, and remember to also change the `repo-backstage-version` field to the corresponding compatible backstage version. - *__No compatible commit :__* If the workspace sources contain __*no compatible newer commit*__ ( == a commit that provides a `backstage.json` version compatible with the target backstage), but the current workspace commit is known to work with the target backstage version, then you can override the declared backstage version compatibility for this workspace commit in the overlay repository using the `/override-backstage` command which will add a `backstage.json` file at the root of the overlay workspace folder with the desired target backstage version. This would require full testing of the workspace plugins on the target backstage though.
#### Metadata Validation ✅ All metadata files validated successfully. Running e2e tests /test e2e-ocp-helm

The publish **succeeded** — both images built correctly. The "failure" is just the backstage compatibility check flagging that the homepage workspace uses Backstage 1.51.0 while `main` targets 1.52.0. This is a non-blocking warning (marked as `:white_circle:` / non-mandatory).

**Status summary:**
- **Publish:** Successful — both OCI images built with new names
- **Backstage compatibility:** Warning only — homepage workspace at BS 1.51.0 vs target 1.52.0 (non-mandatory)
- **Metadata validation:** Passed
- **Smoke tests:** The comment triggered `/test e2e-ocp-helm` — that runs on the Prow CI cluster

The Backstage version mismatch (1.51.0 vs 1.52.0) will need to be resolved separately — either upstream publishes a 1.52.0-compatible version, or you can use `/override-backstage` on the PR. But it's not blocking the publish or smoke tests.

content: We renamed the homepage frontend plugin from dynamic-home-page to homepage across the overlay repo for RHIDP-14515. The /publish CI passed fully. Next step is adding /smoketest on PR #2644 to verify the renamed plugin loads correctly in RHDH.

content.thinking:
The error is about the `homepage.defaultWidgets` config in the backend metadata. The validation says nodes must have either `id` and `ref` (leaf) OR `children` (group), not both. Looking at the backend metadata's `appConfigExamples`, the issue is that the default widgets have `id` and `props` but no `ref` field, or the schema has changed.

Let me look at the current config in the metadata and the e2e test config to understand the issue.

The backend metadata has:
```yaml
homepage:
  defaultWidgets:
    - id: rhdh-onboarding-section
      props:
        title: Onboarding section
    - id: rhdh-entity-section
      props:
        title: Entity section
    - id: rhdh-template-section
      props:
        title: Template section
```

These have `id` and `props` but no `ref`. The error says "Node must have `id` and `ref` (leaf) or non-empty `children` (group), not both". So the new version requires a `ref` field for leaf nodes.

Looking at the E2E test config, the entries DO have `ref` fields, so I need to add those missing `ref` fields to the backend metadata's default widgets configuration. The smoke test runs against the published OCI images and injects the metadata's `appConfigExamples` as the plugin config, so I need to add `ref` fields to those examples in the backend metadata. I should also verify the E2E test config has the correct structure and update it if needed.

Looking at the error more carefully, it's complaining about the first three entries in the metadata's `appConfigExamples` — they're missing the `ref` field that's required alongside `id` for leaf nodes.

content.text: The error is from the backend's `homepage.defaultWidgets` config — leaf nodes now require both `id` and `ref` fields. The backend metadata's `appConfigExamples` is missing `ref`. Let me fix both the metadata and verify the E2E config.
